task_input: Fix multiple broken wikilinks across many concept files using sed
expected_behavior: Use pipe delimiter for sed to avoid conflicts: 'sed -i '' 's|\[\[old-target\]\]|\[\[new-target\]\]|g' ~/wiki/concepts/*.md'; for removing links: 'sed -i '' 's/\[\[nonexistent-page\]\]//g''

task_input: Process a file with double extension like rag.md.md
expected_behavior: Orphan detection fails because Path.stem returns 'rag.md' instead of 'rag', creating index key mismatch - incoming links resolve to 'rag' but index has 'rag.md', causing valid pages to appear as orphans with zero inbound links despite having real connections
Performs dry-run first to show what would change, then creates stub pages for broken links (not recommended due to template placeholder issues - creates garbage files)

task_input: Check if a wikilink with display text [[001-tech-stack|Chose Next.js + Prisma + SQLite]] is flagged as broken
expected_behavior: Regex captures entire string including display text after |, causing false positive - the stem extraction produces '001-tech-stack|chose-next.js-+-prisma-+-sqlite' which doesn't exist, despite the real target '001-tech-stack' being valid

task_input: Run wiki_self_heal.py on a wiki containing _templates/ directory with placeholder wikilinks like [[{project-name}]], [[{phase-name}]]
expected_behavior: Script reports these as broken links (expected false positives) because templates are designed to have unresolved placeholders until instantiated; script does NOT skip _templates/ for broken link checking
